title: Tangy and Silky French Alsatian Apple Gratin
description: A creamy, caramelized French apple gratin with a crunchy filo pastry crust, inspired by the flavors of Alsace.

ingredients.name: apples
ingredients.notes: Granny Smith or other firm variety

instructions: Peel, core, and slice the apples into thin wedges.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the sugar, flour, and cinnamon.
Add the sliced apples to the bowl and toss to coat with the sugar mixture.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the cream, eggs, and nutmeg.
Pour the cream mixture over the apples and sugar mixture, tossing to coat.
Melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the filo pastry to the melted butter and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Transfer the cooked filo pastry to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Pour the apple mixture into the baking dish and smooth the top.
Top the gratin with the remaining filo pastry, pressing down gently.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the apples are tender.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
